name: How to Calculate and Maximize Your Workers' Comp Settlement After a Fall
description: How to Calculate and Maximize Your Workers' Comp Settlement After a Fall - Boise Idaho

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Securing a fair settlement for a workplace fall requires more than just waiting for the insurance company to send a check. It is an active process that requires strategy. Follow these steps to maximize your recovery.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Never settle your case while you are still in active treatment. You must reach MMI, which is the point where doctors say your condition is stable. If you settle before having surgery, you will be responsible for that surgery's cost out of your own pocket.

step.name: Step 4
step.text: ### Step 2: Get an Independent Impairment Rating

step.name: Step 5
step.text: The insurance company’s doctor will give you a 'Permanent Partial Disability' (PPD) rating. If they say you are 3% disabled but you can barely walk, you need a second opinion. A lawyer can help you find an independent medical examiner to provide a more accurate assessment of how your fall affected your ability to work.

step.name: Step 6
step.text: ### Step 3: Calculate Your Future Medical Needs

step.name: Step 7
step.text: A good settlement doesn't just look at what you've spent; it looks at what you WILL spend. If your fall resulted in a joint injury, you may need a replacement in 10 years. A settlement should include 'future medical allocations' to cover these inevitable costs.

step.name: Step 8
step.text: ### Step 4: Account for Permanent Wage Loss

step.name: Step 9
step.text: If your injury prevents you from returning to your high-paying construction job and you have to take a lower-paying desk job, you may be entitled to 'loss of earning capacity' or vocational rehabilitation benefits.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: Adjusters use sophisticated software to determine the lowest possible payout. You need someone who knows how to challenge their math and present your case to the Labor Commission if they refuse to be fair. Just Work Comp Law specializes in these negotiations, ensuring that 'clear communication' and 'maximum results' aren't just slogans, but your reality.
